channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: San Francisco Orders Tech Giants to Remove AI Nudification Apps
item.description: San Francisco has issued cease-and-desist letters to Apple and Google, demanding the removal of AI-powered apps that generate nonconsensual nude images. The city’s attorney alleges these applications ...

item.title: Supreme Court Clarifies SIR Exclusions, US Strikes Iran's Chabahar Port
item.description: The Supreme Court verbally reiterated that persons excluded as voters during the special intensive revision of electoral rolls will not automatically lose their citizenship. The court emphasized that ...

item.title: Kanga Cup concludes with heartfelt celebrations and community spirit in Canberra
item.description: The Kanga Cup, Australia's largest youth football tournament in the southern hemisphere, concluded its final day in Canberra with a mix of celebration, camaraderie, and sporting excellence. Over 4,400...

item.title: PPP Chairman Calls for AJK Reopening as US-Iran Ceasefire Talks Continue
item.description: Pakistan Peoples Party (PPP) Chairman Bilawal Bhutto-Zardari has urged the federal government to reopen Azad Jammu and Kashmir (AJK), linking it to potential peace efforts between the United States an...

item.title: Gold's shift from momentum trade to safe-haven asset
item.description: The article discusses the declining appeal of gold as a speculative investment and its return to its traditional role as a hedge against economic uncertainty. After a dramatic price surge from $3,000 ...

item.title: King David School Bus Crash Claims 20 Lives, Survivor Describes Terrifying Moments
item.description: A tragic bus crash involving pupils from King David Junior School in Uganda has left 20 children and one adult dead. The incident occurred on July 16 when a school trip bus lost control, veered off th...

item.title: NBI subpoenas PHISGOC officials over alleged SEA Games funding anomalies
item.description: The National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) has announced that it will subpoena key members of the Philippine Southeast Asian Games Organizing Committee (PHISGOC) for its ongoing probe into alleged irr...

item.title: French Billionaire Xavier Niel Becomes Largest Vodafone Shareholder in Africa
item.description: French billionaire Xavier Niel, known for his disruptive ventures in telecoms, is making a significant comeback in Africa by acquiring a major stake in Vodafone. This $5.1bn deal positions him as Voda...

item.title: Banyana Banyana Coach Ellis Announces Wafcon Squad with Blend of Experience and Youth
item.description: Banyana Banyana coach Desiree Ellis has announced a 26-player squad for the 2026 Women’s Africa Cup of Nations (Wafcon), blending seasoned veterans with emerging talent. The squad includes stalwarts l...

item.title: Plateau attack leaves eight cattle dead, several injured
item.description: Gunmen killed eight cattle and injured several others in a fresh attack in Yarwe village, Fan District of Barkin Ladi Local Government Area of Plateau State. State Chairman of Miyetti Allah Cattle Bre...

item.title: African Nations Prefer China Over U.S. Under Trump: Pew Poll
item.description: A new Pew Research Center poll reveals that African nations increasingly favor China over the United States under President Donald Trump's administration. The survey, conducted across multiple countri...

item.title: Nigeria Regulates Virtual Assets with New Council
item.description: President Bola Tinubu has signed an executive order to create a Virtual Asset Council, aiming to harmonize regulations for virtual assets like cryptocurrencies. The order seeks to prevent fraud, prote...

item.title: Challenges Faced by Electrical Pre-Apprenticeship Graduates in Securing Apprenticeships
item.description: Two graduates from an electrical pre-apprenticeship program in London, Ontario, highlight the difficulty of securing an apprenticeship despite completing hands-on training. While programs like North A...

item.title: Faith and Hope in a Turbulent World: Lessons from a Pastor and the Cricket World Cup
item.description: Justin Langer reflects on a chance encounter with George, a Ghanaian pastor, during a trip to Manchester for the Cricket World Cup. Their conversation about faith, hope, and love highlights the univer...

item.title: Parliament to Question School Principals on Capitation Fund Management
item.description: Hundreds of public secondary school principals in Kenya are set to appear before parliament as lawmakers intensify scrutiny over the management of billions of shillings allocated to schools. The Natio...

item.title: Philippine Coast Guard Defends Transparency Efforts Amid China Disputes
item.description: Rear Admiral Jay Tarriela, spokesperson of the Philippine Coast Guard (PCG) for the West Philippine Sea, rejected Senator Robin Padilla's claim that the government's transparency campaign in the area ...

item.title: Sister Mary Kenneth Keller: Pioneering Nun and First Woman to Earn Computer Science Doctorate
item.description: Sister Mary Kenneth Keller (born Evelyn Marie Keller, December 17, 1913 – January 10, 1985) was a pioneering American Catholic nun, educator, and computer scientist. She became the first woman in the ...

item.title: Springboks Tour Squad Finalized Without Richie Mo'unga
item.description: New Zealand head coach Dave Rennie has confirmed that Richie Mo'unga will not be part of the Springboks tour squad for the 'Greatest Rivalry' series against South Africa. The decision follows Mo'unga'...

item.title: India's First Hydrogen-Powered Train Launched in Haryana
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi launched India's first hydrogen-powered fuel cell train from Jind in Haryana, marking a significant step towards sustainable rail transport. The train, part of a global sh...

item.title: Wildfire Smoke and Its Impact on Brain Health: A Growing Health Concern
item.description: Wildfire smoke poses significant risks to brain health beyond its well-known effects on the respiratory and cardiovascular systems. Research indicates that fine particulate matter (PM2.5) from wildfir...
